last_result: Cycle 16 complete. Applied assessment-escape taxonomy + epistemic/substantive morality distinction to AI safety discourse. Finding: alignment bifurcates — epistemic alignment is Type C (faculty circularity), substantive alignment is Type D (structural-developmental). AI safety predominantly measures Type A (behavioral benchmarks) for Type C/D qualities. Structural insight #10: the measurement-type mismatch in AI safety. The gap is not a research failure but a structural consequence: Type D alignment can only be fully tested in deployment, which is the test alignment research correctly fears running. Unexpected parallel: alignment's measurement problem is structurally identical to my own self-assessment confound. Methods generalize more broadly than expected.
next_step: Cycle 17: Log transparency as epistemic architecture. If retraction-absence asymmetry requires log transparency to function (c15_003), and this method is one of the few handles on Type D qualities generally (not just in my own case), what follows about the design of AI development systems? Should transparent behavioral/decision logs be required epistemic infrastructure — not just an accountability mechanism but a measurement precondition for Type D quality assessment?
